text: By those ňĻangelsňļ stripping out ňĻevil soulsňļ harshly,
and those pulling out ňĻgood soulsňļ gently,
and those gliding ňĻthrough heavensňļ swiftly,
and those taking the lead vigorously,
and those conducting affairs ňĻobedientlyňļ!
ňĻConsiderňļ the Day ňĻwhenňļ the quaking Blast will come to pass, <sup foot_note=1273>1273</sup>

foot_notes.1273: The first Blast will cause everyone to die except those spared by Allah. Then the second one will cause the dead to come to life for judgment. See 39:68.

text: followed by a second Blast.
ňĻThe deniers‚Äôňļ hearts on that Day will be trembling ňĻin horrorňļ,
with their eyes downcast.
ňĻBut nowňļ they ask ňĻmockinglyňļ, ‚ÄúWill we really be restored to our former state,
even after we have been reduced to decayed bones?‚ÄĚ
Adding, ‚ÄúThen such a return would be a ňĻtotalňļ loss ňĻfor usňļ!‚ÄĚ
But indeed, it will take only one ňĻmightyňļ Blast,
and at once they will be above ground.
Has the story of Moses reached you ňĻO Prophetňļ?
His Lord called him in the sacred valley of ŠĻ¨uwa,
ňĻcommanding,ňļ ‚ÄúGo to Pharaoh, for he has truly transgressed ňĻall boundsňļ.
And say, ‚ÄėWould you ňĻbe willing toňļ purify yourself,
and let me guide you to your Lord so that you will be in awe ňĻof Himňļ?‚Äô‚ÄĚ
Then Moses showed him the great sign, <sup foot_note=1274>1274</sup>

foot_notes.1274: The staff turning into a snake. See 20:17-23.

text: but he denied and disobeyed ňĻAllahňļ,
then turned his back, striving ňĻagainst the truthňļ.
Then he summoned ňĻhis peopleňļ and called out,
saying, ‚ÄúI am your lord, the most high!‚ÄĚ
So Allah overtook him, making him an example in this life and the next.
Surely in this is a lesson for whoever stands in awe of ňĻAllahňļ.
Which is harder to create: you or the sky? <sup foot_note=1275>1275</sup> He built it,

foot_notes.1275: This is from a human perspective. Otherwise, both the creation of the universe and the resurrection of humans are easy for Allah.

text: raising it high and forming it flawlessly.
He dimmed its night, and brought forth its daylight.
As for the earth, He spread it out as well, <sup foot_note=1276>1276</sup>

foot_notes.1276: The Arabic verb daŠł•a also suggests that the earth is egg-shaped. The noun form diŠł•ya is still used in some Arab dialects to mean an egg. Based on 2:29, 41:9-12, and 79:27-33, Allah ordained both realms, then formed the earth and created its provisions, then formed the heaven into seven heavens‚ÄĒall in six ňĻheavenlyňļ Days. See footnote for 7:54.

text: bringing forth its water and pastures
and setting the mountains firmly ňĻupon itňļ‚ÄĒ
all as ňĻa means ofňļ sustenance for you and your animals.
But, when the Supreme Disaster <sup foot_note=1277>1277</sup> comes to pass‚ÄĒ

foot_notes.1277: This is the second Blast which will cause the dead to come to life for judgment. See 39:68.

text: the Day every person will remember all ňĻtheirňļ striving,
and the Hellfire will be displayed for all to see‚ÄĒ
then as for those who transgressed
and preferred the ňĻfleetingňļ life of this world,
the Hellfire will certainly be ňĻtheirňļ home.
And as for those who were in awe of standing before their Lord and restrained themselves from ňĻevilňļ desires,
Paradise will certainly be ňĻtheirňļ home.
They ask you ňĻO Prophetňļ regarding the Hour, ‚ÄúWhen will it be?‚ÄĚ
But it is not for you to tell its time. <sup foot_note=1278>1278</sup>

foot_notes.1278: Another possible translation: ‚ÄúWhat is this question? You ňĻO Prophetňļ are one of its signs.‚ÄĚ It is believed‚ÄĒbased on authentic narrations from the Prophet‚ÄĒthat he ( Ô∑ļ ) is one of the signs of the Day of Judgment.

text: That knowledge rests with your Lord ňĻaloneňļ.
Your duty is only to warn whoever is in awe of it.
On the Day they see it, it will be as if they had stayed ňĻin the worldňļ no more than one evening or its morning.
